title: Reading Chess Notation
summary: Decode any chess move in algebraic notation

steps.title: The System
steps.text: Algebraic notation describes every move uniquely. Files (columns) are letters a–h, ranks (rows) are numbers 1–8. White's first rank is 1, Black's is 8. A square is file+rank: e4, d5, h8.

steps.title: Piece Letters
steps.text: Pieces are K (King), Q (Queen), R (Rook), B (Bishop), N (Knight). Pawns have no letter — "e4" alone means a pawn to e4. Captures use "x": Nxe5 means knight captures on e5.

steps.title: Special Symbols
steps.text: "+" means check. "#" means checkmate. "O-O" is short castle, "O-O-O" is long castle. "=" marks promotion: e8=Q (pawn promotes to queen). When two pieces could move to the same square, the starting file or rank is added: Nbd2 or R1e1.

steps.hint: Just select the pawn on e2 and move it to e4.
The knight from g1 jumps to f3.
